response.justification: While Rodriguez demonstrates strong general software development expertise with 15+ years of experience, this role requires native-level Japanese language proficiency (not listed) and specialized expertise in embedded security and networking that is entirely absent from the resume. Their C/embedded systems experience at National Instruments ended in 2015 and their career has since moved toward web, mobile, and AI stacks—away from the embedded firmware and security domains required. The candidate is fundamentally misaligned with the technical specialization needed.
strengths: 15+ years of software development experience across multiple domains, well exceeding the 3-year minimum
C/C++ experience on embedded systems at National Instruments, with demonstrated ability to work on complex, low-level platform development
Strong Linux knowledge and system-level infrastructure experience, suggesting capability to work with kernel source and embedded environments
concerns: No native-level Japanese language proficiency listed; resume shows only English (fluent), Romanian (fluent), and German (basic), making this a critical blocker for a position explicitly requiring Japanese
Complete absence of security domain expertise—no mention of encryption, authentication systems (802.1X, Captive Portal), MACsec, tamper detection, or any security technologies required by the role
No embedded networking or switch expertise; career focused on web, mobile, and AI/DevOps, with relevant embedded C work ending in 2015 (11 years ago), indicating significant domain drift

key_factors.factor: No security or embedded networking domain expertise
